name: Low-dose TA-65 increased leukocyte telomere length in a human RCT
summary: The strongest direct human telomerase-restoration signal is a one-year TA-65 randomized trial with telomere-length endpoints.
statement: In relatively healthy CMV-positive older adults, low-dose TA-65 increased leukocyte telomere length over 12 months compared with baseline while placebo lost telomere length.
population_or_model: Relatively healthy CMV-positive adults aged 53 to 87 years
quantitative_note: The low-dose group increased telomere length by 530 +/- 180 bp (P=0.005), while placebo lost 290 +/- 100 bp (P=0.01); the high-dose group trend did not reach statistical significance.
caveats: The endpoint is leukocyte telomere length, not function, disease prevention, or survival.
The dose-response pattern was not straightforward.
The study had commercial conflicts: some authors were employees or consultants of T.A. Sciences.
